name: TestJourney

unions.name: EffectHandlerResult
unions.comment: The results returned by [`handleEffect`](#handleEffect). Returns either an `EffectProcessed` w/ an `Expectation` and the msg to be sent to the `update` function of the application under test.

aliases.name: ProgramDefinition
aliases.comment:
Defines the application under test. This mirrors your elm `Program`, but without subscriptions or init, and `List effect` instead of `Cmd`.

`debugToString` should always be `Debug.toString`. Having the caller supply this allows `elm-test-program` to avoid it's use, and be published as a package.
aliases.type: { view : model -> Browser.Document msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, List.List effect ), initialModel : model, debugToString : effect -> String.String }

values.name: blur
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a blur event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: check
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a check true event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: click
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a click event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: custom
values.comment:
Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a custom, and processes the generated message.

                        See (see [Test.Html.Event.custom](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/test/latest/Test-Html-Event#custom))
values.type: String.String -> Json.Encode.Value -> TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: dontSee
values.comment:
Expect a given element to not exist. If the target is a sequence of page finders (e.g. `parent.child.grandchild`), `dontSee` only passes if
`parent`, and `child` exist, but `grandchild` does not. Otherwise, it's too easy for `dontSee` to inadvertantly pass.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: doubleClick
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a double-click event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: expectModel
values.comment: Directly run an expectation on the current model of the application under tests. Useful when there are changes that aren't represented in the UI (e.g. I want to make sure I get a new session id after logging in).
values.type: (model -> Expect.Expectation) -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: finish
values.comment:
Call at the end of your test. Automatically add an expectations that there are no
pending effects left to process, since that is usually inadvertant in practice.
values.type: TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: focus
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a focus event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: handleEffect
values.comment:
Processes the next effect waiting to be processed.

This is usually simulating the other end of your effect. In the case of an effect representing an HTTP Cmd, this takes place of the server. For an out port, this plays the role simulating the javascript code.

        -- Application Under Test
        type alias ItemID = Int

        type Msg =
            ItemAdded ItemID

        type MyEffect =
            EffectAddItem (Result Http.Error ItemID) String
            EffectRemoveItem ItemID


        -- Test
        handleAddItem effect =
            case effect of
                EffectAddItem msg label ->
                    EffectProcessed
                        (Expect.equal
                            input
                            "myLabel"
                        )
                        (msg
                            (Ok ItemID 55))
                        )

                _ ->
                    EffectUnexpected
values.type: (effect -> TestJourney.EffectHandlerResult msg) -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: injectMsg
values.comment: Sends the supplied `msg` into appliication under test. This is how `ports` and `Subscriptions` should be simulated.
values.type: msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: input
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a input event w/ the given text, and processes the generated message.
values.type: String.String -> TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mapModel
values.comment: Directly transform the underlying model. This can be useful for altering your [`initialModel`](#ProgramDefinition) supplied in [`ProgramDefinition`](#ProgramDefinition). This should generally be avoided, in the body of your tests.
values.type: (model -> model) -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseDown
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseDown event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseEnter
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseEnter event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseLeave
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseLeave event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseOut
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseOut event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseOver
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseOver event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: mouseUp
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a mouseUp event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: see
values.comment: Expect a given element to exist exactly once.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: seeCount
values.comment: Expect exactly a given number of matching elements to exist.
values.type: Basics.Int -> (Basics.Int -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: seeText
values.comment: Expect a given element to have a descendant w/ the given text.
values.type: String.String -> TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: start
values.comment: Call at the start of your test pipeline to kick things off.
values.type: TestJourney.ProgramDefinition model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: submit
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates a submit event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

values.name: uncheck
values.comment: Ensures the target element exists exactly once, simulates an check false event, and processes the generated message.
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Element children -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg -> TestJourney.ProgramState model effect msg

name: TestJourney.Page
comment:
Create Page Objects for `elm-test-journey`.


## Building the Page Model

@docs root, multiple, multipleRecord, single, singleRecord


## Types

@docs Element, Finder


## Helpers

In practice, a convention like "all elements are identified by an `"data-test"="myTestIdentifier"`" makes page objects
more resiliant, and makes it clear when a node in the application under test has test dependencies. These helpers make
creating Page Objects targeting that convention simpler. If your project has it's own conventions, create your own set of
helpers.

@docs multipleTestAttr, multipleRecordTestAttr, singleTestAttr, singleRecordTestAttr

aliases.name: Element
aliases.comment: An element represents a series of selectors and functions that result in finding a single specific node in your application under test. When defining a record element, via [`singleRecord`](#singleRecord), or [`multipleRecord`](#multipleRecord), ensure that you always have a field named `self`. `self` allows elements with and without other fields defined to be treated uniformly.

aliases.name: Finder
aliases.comment: Used by [`TestJourney` ](../../TestJourney.elm) to help locate [`Elements`](#Element) defined in your page.

values.name: multiple
values.comment:
Represents a list of nodes.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myListItem =
                    multiple rootElement
                        "myListItem"
                        [ Selector.attribute (Attributes.attribute "data-test" "myListItem")
                        ]
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myListItem 1
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Internal.FriendlyName -> List.List Test.Html.Selector.Selector -> Basics.Int -> TestJourney.Page.Finder

values.name: multipleRecord
values.comment:
Represents a list of nodes w/ custom children.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myListItem =
                    multipleRecord rootElement
                        "myListItem"
                        [ Selector.attribute
                            (Attributes.attribute "data-test" "myListItem")
                        ]
                        (\myListItem ->
                            { self = myListItem
                            , label = singleTestAttr myListItem "itemLabel"
                            }
                        )
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myListItem 0 |> .label
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Internal.FriendlyName -> List.List Test.Html.Selector.Selector -> (TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> Basics.Int -> TestJourney.Page.Element children

values.name: multipleRecordTestAttr
values.comment:
Represents a list of nodes w/ custom children, following the "data-test" attribute convention.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myListItem =
                    multipleRecord rootElement
                        "myListItem"
                        (\myListItem ->
                            { self = myListItem
                            , label = singleTestAttr myListItem "itemLabel"
                            }
                        )
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myListItem 0 |> .label
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> String.String -> (TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> Basics.Int -> TestJourney.Page.Element children

values.name: multipleTestAttr
values.comment:
Represents a list of nodes, following the "data-test" attribute convention.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myListItem =
                    multipleTestAttr rootElement "myListItem"
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myListItem 1
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> String.String -> Basics.Int -> TestJourney.Page.Finder

values.name: root
values.comment:
Sets up the context for the rest of the page object. An empty selector will match the root of the view (usually the body tag).

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myChildElement = singleTestAttr rootElement "myChildElement"
                }
            )
values.type: List.List Test.Html.Selector.Selector -> (TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> TestJourney.Page.Element children

values.name: single
values.comment:
Creates an ['Element'](#Element) referencing a single node.

    page =
        root
            []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myChildElement = single rootElement "myChildElement" [ Selector.attribute (Attributes.attribute "data-test" "myChildElement") ]
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myChildElement
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Internal.FriendlyName -> List.List Test.Html.Selector.Selector -> TestJourney.Page.Element {}

values.name: singleRecord
values.comment:
Creates an ['Element'](#Element) referencing a single node, w/ custom children.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myElementWithChildren =
                    singleRecord "myChildElement"
                        [ Selector.attribute (Attributes.attribute "data-test" "myChildElement") ]
                        (\myElementWithChildren ->
                            { self = myElementWithChildren
                            , childOfChild = singleTestAttr myElementWithChildren "childOfChild"
                            }
                        )
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myElementWithChildren.childOfChild
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Internal.FriendlyName -> List.List Test.Html.Selector.Selector -> (TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> TestJourney.Page.Element children

values.name: singleRecordTestAttr
values.comment:
Creates an ['Element'](#Element) referencing a single node, w/ custom children, following the "data-test" attribute convention.

    page =
        root []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myElementWithChildren =
                    singleRecord "myChildElement"
                        (\myElementWithChildren ->
                            { self = myElementWithChildren
                            , childOfChild = singleTestAttr myElementWithChildren "childOfChild"
                            }
                        )
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myElementWithChildren.childOfChild
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> String.String -> (TestJourney.Page.Finder -> TestJourney.Page.Element children) -> TestJourney.Page.Element children

values.name: singleTestAttr
values.comment:
Creates an ['Element'](#Element) referencing a single node, following the "data-test" attribute convention.

    page =
        root
            []
            (\rootElement ->
                { self = rootElement
                , myChildElement = singleTestAttr rootElement "myChildElement"
                }
            )

    element =
        page.myChildElement
values.type: TestJourney.Page.Finder -> String.String -> TestJourney.Page.Element {}
